name: Dark Narratives
description: Anime, fiction, and geopolitics for people who think moral clarity is overrated. We watch Berserk and talk about power, complicity, and what stories owe their characters.
vibe: No hot takes. Dense threads. People who read the manga.

messages.text: ok hot take: Griffith's arc is more coherent than Thorfinn's because the corruption was always there
disagree. Thorfinn had the arc of someone who didn't get to choose their corruption. that's different
both are also just HOI4 playthroughs at a narrative level
the Griffith debate is actually a good lens for thinking about technocracy in Iran — people who genuinely believe they're the only competent option
there's a mod for this scenario. someone made an Iran 1979 start for HOI4
linking it. but also this is exactly the thread I came here for

summary: Dense discussion group for people who watch dark, morally complex anime and also read geopolitics and strategy. The tone is unhurried and analytical — threads connect fiction to real power dynamics, and references to HOI4 scenarios and Iranian politics coexist naturally.

name: Rough Draft Builders
description: Engineers and non-engineers who ship unfinished things for real people. No roadmaps, no sprints — just builders who believe a working prototype beats a polished deck.
vibe: Ship first. Explain later. Celebrate the ugly MVP.

messages.text: shipped the housing benefit guide last night. it's ugly and it works. link in thread
ok the USSR tank progression tracker i built in War Thunder data is also ugly and also works
genuinely could not tell which project you were referring to for a second
i recorded a rough guitar demo while the CI was running. sending to group
next week: demo night. ugliest first version wins a session of honest roasting
i'm in. my code still has console.logs in prod. i'll win

summary: A show-and-tell community for builders who ship rough, functional prototypes. Cloud engineers, hobbyist musicians, and strategy game players coexist — what connects them is a bias toward making over perfecting. Demo nights celebrate broken things that work.

name: Cloud Craft
description: Platform engineers and cloud architects who care about the craft, not the cert count. We talk GCP, Kubernetes, architecture decisions, and occasionally argue about whether Terraform is overengineered.
vibe: Craft over career. Privacy-first by default. No vendor worship.

messages.text: writing up the Lloyds-style infra consolidation pattern I've been running. posting a draft thread later
been waiting for this. the Anthos question is live — is it actually worth it or just expensive Kubernetes with a Google label
expensive Kubernetes with a Google label. I said what I said
disagree. the managed control plane alone saved us three sprints
context: what's your node count and do you span regions
posted the Istio cert notes in resources if anyone's prepping
draft thread is up. tl;dr: consolidation is worth it only if you standardise networking first, not after

summary: Craft-focused cloud engineering community for people who care about architecture quality and privacy-first design over career signalling. Threads go deep on GCP, Kubernetes, and consolidation decisions. The tone is collegial but precise — people cite specific contexts before making claims.
